steps.title: Identify the charity with the greatest percent of expenses on programs
steps.content: Scan the scatterplot for the highest data point on the y-axis. The topmost point lies at approximately $(3{,}400, 92)$ — about \$3.4 billion in total income with roughly 92% of expenses on programs. This is the charity we need to analyze.

steps.title: Read the predicted value from the line of best fit
steps.content: At $x \approx 3{,}400$, trace vertically up to the line of best fit and read the corresponding $y$-value. The line passes through this region at approximately $y = 85$. This is the model's predicted percent.

steps.title: Calculate the residual (actual minus predicted)
steps.content:
The residual is the actual value minus the predicted value:
$$92 - 85 = 7$$
The actual percent lies 7 percentage points above the predicted value. That matches choice B — B is correct.
